factors.name: Mega-cap tech sentiment
factors.summary: Tesla +8% on self-driving update; AeroVironment +19% on earnings beat; Strategy +12% on capital plan. Single-name momentum in QQQ heavyweights supporting broader NDX.

factors.name: Fed independence affirmation
factors.summary: Supreme Court ruled in favor of Fed independence vs. Trump removal attempts. Reduces near-term regulatory uncertainty for tech mega-caps and reduces tail risk of political interference in monetary policy.

factors.name: AI capex and robotics narrative
factors.summary: Waymo-DoorDash autonomous delivery expansion; Rocket Lab acquiring Iridium for $8B; Salesforce on AI buying spree (3 deals in June). Hyperscaler AI investment theme intact.

factors.name: Treasury yields steady
factors.summary: US 10Y yields flat at 4.37%; no surprise inflation print or Fed repricing event. Long-duration growth multiples stable, no tailwind or headwind to valuations.

factors.name: Dollar strength (USD breakout)
factors.summary: DXY broke 40-year trading range; USD/JPY at 161.97 (40-year high). Strong dollar headwind to multinational tech earnings and international revenue drag on NQ components.

factors.name: Real yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data released; gold down $72 to ~$4,015 suggests real yields stable-to-slightly-higher. Modest headwind to long-duration tech, but not acute.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk easing
factors.summary: US-Iran interim deal; talks scheduled for Qatar Tuesday. Oil prices rising but tension-driven volatility subsiding. Risk-on sentiment supporting equities, reduces tail VIX expansion risk.

factors.name: China macro weakness
factors.summary: Bloomberg: China stocks lagging most since 2001 despite AI advances; PBoC new overnight facility dovish signal at 1.25%. China growth concerns weigh on commodity, EEM, and semi-cyclical demand.

factors.name: Japan data miss
factors.summary: May industrial production +0.5% m/m vs. +1.1% expected; -1.7% y/y. Weak yen (161.97) not translating to export strength. Regional growth concerns.

factors.name: Regulatory tailwind (Trump court wins)
factors.summary: Supreme Court allows Trump to fire FTC/SEC commissioners at will, overturning Humphrey's Executor precedent. Reduces regulatory friction for big tech (antitrust concern lower near-term); crypto regulation also eases with executive discretion.

factors.name: Crypto correlation volatility
factors.summary: Bitcoin at $60K with put-call ratio at 1-year high (bear positioning); ETF outflows persist. BTC-NQ linkage risk-on/off, but not decisively breakout-bullish. Noise around Strategy MSTR volatility.

factors.name: Earnings visibility near-term
factors.summary: No major mega-cap earnings releases in imminent 24-72h news window. Forward guidance and Q3 outlook remain key unknowns until earnings season; current move driven by sentiment and positioning, not fundamentals.

screening.narrative: NAS100 rallying +1.72% on the back of positive mega-cap tech sentiment (Tesla, AeroVironment, Strategy), Supreme Court affirmation of Fed independence, and geopolitical risk de-escalation (US-Iran interim deal). The move is in Phase 2 — underway but not yet extended — supported by AI narrative staying intact (Salesforce, Rocket Lab M&A) and regulatory tail risks diminishing. However, strong USD (DXY breakout, USD/JPY 40-year high) creates a meaningful headwind for international NQ revenue, and China macro weakness (Bloomberg report on stock underperformance) raises cyclical growth concerns. Japan data miss and dovish PBoC signals suggest regional demand softening.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (jobs report) — likely Friday, could reprice Fed rate expectations
RBA minutes Tuesday (overnight Asian session) — hints at tightening bias; AUD-sensitive, cross-flows into NQ
China official PMI (Tuesday, Asian hours) — manufacturing health critical for semiconductor demand
ECB Forum on Central Banking (already started Monday, continues) — Lagarde signaling dovish hold; EUR weakness = USD strength = NQ headwind
FOMC minutes or any Fed speaker commentary — rate path clarity
Mega-cap tech earnings calendar — forward guidance on cloud capex, AI demand, margin outlook
screening.risk_warning: Move is already +1.72% in 24h and +0.16% in 4h; if this rally extends another 1%+ without retest, late-stage positioning risk increases. Sharp USD strength could surprise downside on multinational tech earnings revisions. China growth concerns (PMI Tuesday) could spark rotation out of growth/mega-cap tech into value/defensives. FOMC or inflation surprise could reprice Treasury yields upward, crushing long-duration multiples. Geopolitical risk remains live (Iran talks fragile); any escalation resets risk appetite sharply lower.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 and moderately extended; avoid chasing at current levels. Optimal entry on pullback to 29,700–29,750 (support), or wait for Fed/inflation clarity before adding. If break above 30,000, next resistance 30,100–30,200, but insufficient confluence to warrant new longs here.

factors.name: China PMI beat (Manufacturing 50.3 vs 50.1 exp)
factors.summary: China factory PMI exceeded expectations at 50.3, driven by AI-linked export demand. This supports risk-on sentiment and benefits NDX mega-caps with heavy China exposure (AAPL, MSFT, TSMC supply chain).

factors.name: Tech sector rebound narrative
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'US Tech Rebounds' with Asia trade showing strong inflows into tech on AI buildout optimism. Mega-cap FAANG names positioned to benefit from sustained capex cycles.

factors.name: USD strength / DXY above 101.30
factors.summary: USD Index strengthens above 101.00 on Fed rate hike bets, which pressures duration-sensitive NDX constituents and international revenue streams. Inverse correlation risk for growth tech.

factors.name: Yen intervention risk (USD/JPY 162.40, 40-year low)
factors.summary: Japanese yen hits weakest level since 1986; Japanese officials signal readiness for intervention at any time. Sudden BOJ/MOF action could trigger sharp yen rally, dollar weakness, and risk-asset pullback.

factors.name: Real yields / inflation repricing
factors.summary: Gold drops below $4,000 on inflation concerns and higher real rate expectations. Long-duration tech (NVDA, TSLA, MSFT) sensitive to real yield compression; current backdrop suggests sticky inflation fears limiting multiple expansion.

factors.name: Oil price stabilization (WTI ~$70, down from $70+)
factors.summary: Oil retreats as Iran-US talks progress and Hormuz reopens; Morgan Stanley warns of glut risk. Lower energy costs ease margin pressure for tech but reduce geopolitical risk premium.

factors.name: Crypto weakness (Bitcoin sub-$60k, Ether/Solana slide)
factors.summary: CoinDesk reports crypto pullback on Strategy's bitcoin sales plan and yen weakness. NAS100 has shown BTC correlation on risk-off days; weakness here signals underlying risk-asset caution.

factors.name: 24h price action: +1.72% move already in place
factors.summary: NDX already rallied 1.72% in 24h, suggesting move is in early PHASE_2. Further upside may face profit-taking unless confirmed by strong earnings or macro catalyst.

factors.name: No major earnings or Fed event imminent (next 48h)
factors.summary: Calendar clear near-term; next high-impact catalysts likely CPI, NFP, or FOMC in next 1-2 weeks. Lack of immediate catalyst caps conviction.

factors.name: Antitrust / regulatory overhang (Apple India, Amazon Australia)
factors.summary: Minor but ongoing regulatory pressure on mega-caps (AAPL, AMZN). Supreme Court DMA clarity eases some concern but does not eliminate medium-term risk.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 has rallied 1.72% in 24h on China PMI beat and tech sentiment rebound driven by AI narrative. However, rally faces headwinds from strengthening USD (DXY 101.30), real yield pressure (gold below $4000), and yen intervention risk (USD/JPY at 40-year high near 162.40). Crypto weakness and crypto-stock correlation suggest underlying risk appetite is fragile. Move is in PHASE_2; further upside momentum depends on either a major positive catalyst (strong mega-cap earnings, dovish Fed surprise) or a pullback to retest support, which would offer a cleaner entry.
catalysts_ahead: US Jobs Report (NFP) — likely early July; inflation-sensitive, impacts Fed rate expectations
ECB rate decision on July inflation surprise — sources flag ECB could move lower if June CPI is weak
BOJ board member Sato speech (0800 GMT today) — potential hawkish surprise could strengthen yen, trigger intervention, and reverse risk-on rally
Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon earnings — typically move NDX 1–3% each; awaited for cloud capex guidance
US-Iran talks in Doha (ongoing today) — geopolitical resolution or escalation could reprice oil and risk sentiment
FOMC decision (mid-July) — major repricing event for long-duration growth multiples
screening.risk_warning: Yen intervention would likely trigger sharp risk-off reversal and NAS100 pullback 1–2%. Real yield spike (10Y yield >4.5%) or inflation surprise could compress NDX multiples. Earnings miss from mega-cap (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL) or weak hyperscaler capex guidance would invalidate AI rally narrative and sink index. Strong USD continuation (DXY >102) also headwinds for international-heavy tech revenue.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already up 1.72% in 24h — avoid chasing above 29900. Wait for pullback to 29500–29650 support or confirm break above 30000 resistance on strong macro catalyst (earnings beat, dovish Fed signal) before scaling long. High risk of profit-taking or yen intervention shock before next significant catalyst.

factors.name: Tech sector momentum & chip strength
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports chip stocks heading for 'best quarter ever' driven by AI capex demand. Nasdaq 100 futures up 0.20% in European hours, outperforming S&P 500 and Dow futures, suggesting tech leadership intact.

factors.name: Inflation cooling in Europe (France)
factors.summary: French CPI unexpectedly fell to 2.0% HICP (+2.0% vs +2.3% expected per Investinglive), cooling ECB hike pressure. Energy price retreat providing relief, which typically supports risk-on and growth multiples.

factors.name: ECB rate-hike uncertainty (Sept possible, not July)
factors.summary: ECB policymaker Wunsch stated hike case 'not as strong now' and Sleijpen emphasized energy retreat will cool inflation. This reduces near-term tightening risk, supporting long-duration growth stocks (NAS100 heavy in those).

factors.name: Real yields and 10Y Treasury direction
factors.summary: No fresh Treasury data in window, but cooler inflation readings typically support lower real yields medium-term. However, USD strength on hawkish Fed backdrop (Japanese yen at 40-year low) could cap downside in yields.

factors.name: Dollar strength and international revenue headwind
factors.summary: USD/JPY broken above 162.00 (40-year high per FXStreet), reflecting broad dollar rally on Fed hawkishness. This pressures international earnings exposure for mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN all have ~40-50% offshore revenue).

factors.name: Fed policy: rate-hike debate ongoing
factors.summary: No fresh FOMC statement in window. Market remains data-dependent. Softer inflation (France, Germany regional data) may ease September hike expectations, but Fed still seen as 'hawkish' by HSBC/MUFG commentary on dollar rally.

factors.name: Q2 earnings season imminent; mega-cap tech guidance critical
factors.summary: No earnings released yet in this window. AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA earnings due in coming weeks; capex guidance on AI/cloud will be the single largest mover for NAS100. Timing uncertainty = lower confidence.

factors.name: Geopolitical relief: US-Iran Hormuz de-escalation
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley cuts oil-price target as Strait of Hormuz reopening faster than expected. Lower energy costs reduce input price pressures and household inflation expectations, supporting consumption and equity multiples.

factors.name: Options & positioning: quant fund selloff last week
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports quant funds suffered 'crippling trading rout' this year as momentum stocks flopped. Suggests recent vol spike and positioning squeeze; NAS100 may face continued rebalancing/hedging pressure into quarter-end.

factors.name: Macro sentiment: risk-on tone but cycle extended
factors.summary: Deutsche Bank notes 'solid risk-on' in US equities with Mag 7 outperformance, helping S&P 500 snap 5-day losing streak. However, recent volatility, quant unwinds, and extended rally (S&P on track for best quarter in 6 years) suggest caution on chase entries.

key_levels.resistance: 30000–30110 (round number, prior session high, NQ futures peak in European hours)

screening.narrative: NAS100 bounced +1.72% over 24h and +0.16% in 4h, driven by tech leadership, softer European inflation (France HICP +2.0%), and easing ECB tightening fears. Energy retreat from Hormuz de-escalation is removing a key macro headwind. However, broad USD strength (yen at 40-year low, Fed hawkishness narrative) pressures international earnings, and recent quant-fund volatility suggests positioning fragility. The move is in early Phase 2—underway but not overextended—with mega-cap tech earnings guidance (coming weeks) as the dominant unknown catalyst.
catalysts_ahead: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA earnings & capex guidance (early July expected)
FOMC decision & dot-plot (late July, but market pricing ongoing)
US Nonfarm Payrolls (first Friday of July, likely 2026-07-03)
US PCE CPI (mid-late July), core inflation track
ECB September rate-hike decision (confirmation or reversal of June guidance)
China GDP, industrial production (mid-July, tech supply chain view)
TSMC, Samsung earnings and wafer capacity outlook
screening.risk_warning: Risk 1: Mega-cap tech earnings miss guidance (especially capex/cloud demand) → 2-3% NAS100 drop. Risk 2: Fed remains hawkish despite softer inflation; real yields rise, growth multiples compress. Risk 3: USD rally continues on Fed hawkishness, offsetting international revenue gains. Risk 4: Geopolitical escalation (Iran-US tensions re-ignite) → energy spike, inflation surprise, rate hikes resume. Risk 5: Quarter-end positioning unwind and options expiry (EUR/USD 1.1400 large expiry noted) could trigger sharp intra-day moves.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress (+1.72% 24h, +0.16% 4h). Wait for retest of 29500–29600 support or consolidation at 29800–29900 before fresh longs; avoid chasing into resistance at 30000. If breaks 30000 with volume, next target 30200–30300 before earnings clarity. Reduce confidence further if dollar index breaks above recent highs into earnings blackout.

factors.name: Q2 2026 earnings strength
factors.summary: US stocks heading for best quarter in six years (Bloomberg 11:40). Wells Fargo and HSBC strategists expect summer rally tailwinds post-rebalancing volatility.

factors.name: Tariff headwind removal
factors.summary: Supreme Court unwound Trump tariffs, creating 'market tailwind no one is talking about' per Bloomberg 11:54, potentially unlocking earnings upside for affected mega-caps.

factors.name: Small-cap outperformance shift
factors.summary: Small-caps enjoying best first half in 35 years (Bloomberg 12:01), but NDX is large-cap concentrated, so marginal direct impact on Nasdaq 100 composition.

factors.name: Fed rate-hike repricing risk
factors.summary: Inflation data shows Germany CPI +2.3% vs +2.6% expected, but core still elevated at +2.5%. Market is pricing 31% July hike odds and 64% September odds—real yields remain sticky and threaten growth multiple compression.

factors.name: Emerging market rotation concerns
factors.summary: BlackRock downgraded EM equities citing AI risks (11:00); this signals potential growth-trade consolidation and could redirect flows away from FAANG-heavy indices into defensive havens.

factors.name: Mag-7 / large-cap tech stress
factors.summary: Mag-7 stocks lost $2.3 trillion in June (11:58 headline). NAS100 is 45%+ concentrated in mega-cap tech; a $2.3T monthly loss signals accumulation of distribution and profit-taking pressure.

factors.name: AI trade resilience debate
factors.summary: MarketWatch 11:51 notes 'big surprise could be AI trade powering higher if valuations climb again,' but also warns of 'persistent scrutiny.' Sentiment mixed; no clear catalyst for re-acceleration yet.

factors.name: USD strength and DXY rebound
factors.summary: USD Index snapped three-day losing streak and traded 0.3% higher to 101.40 (08:52). Stronger USD headwind for NAS100 multinationals' international revenue translation.

factors.name: Gold breakdown below $4,000
factors.summary: Gold fell to seven-month low of $3,941 then recovered to ~$4,028 (11:26). Real-yield support collapsed; this signals renewed Fed hawkishness and higher real yields ahead, growth multiple pressure for NAS100.

factors.name: Momentum/quant fund rout
factors.summary: Quant funds suffered 'most crippling trading rout this year' (08:58) as momentum stocks flopped. Systematic deleveraging and gamma unwinds could exacerbate volatility into quarter-end.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is consolidating after a strong Q2 (+1.72% in 24h) that put it on track for its best quarter in six years. However, the Mag-7 lost $2.3 trillion in June, signaling distribution and profit-taking. The tariff relief from SCOTUS is supportive, and Wells Fargo expects a summer rally, but real yields remain elevated due to sticky core inflation (Germany core +2.5%), and the Fed is not off the table—31% July, 64% September hike odds. Gold's breakdown below $4,000 suggests investor confidence in higher-for-longer rates, which is a headwind for growth multiples. Quant fund distress and momentum wreck add near-term chop and gamma risk.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (July 1, early July) — major Fed re-pricing trigger
US CPI release — real yield re-anchor risk
FOMC meeting (likely early July) — rate-hold vs. hike signaling
NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL earnings pre-guidance (H2 2026) — hyperscaler capex commentary
Fed officials' speeches (Wunsch, Nagel notes signal ECB may hike further) — global hawkishness spillover
Treasury 10Y yield repricing on inflation data — direct impact on NAS100 duration
Iran–US talks resolution (ongoing) — oil supply, stagflation risk, rate-hike risk
screening.risk_warning: If July NFP comes in hot or CPI surprises higher, Fed will be forced to signal July/September hike and real yields will spike further. This would trigger sharp growth-multiple compression and unwind the 1.72% 24h gain. Also, if Mag-7 earnings disappoint on capex guidance or AI demand slowdown becomes evident, NAS100 could break support into low 29000s. Geopolitical escalation (Iran-US talks fail, Hormuz closure) would inflate oil and inflation expectations, compressing growth trades aggressively.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in progress (PHASE 2, +1.72% in 24h after soft Germany CPI). Do not chase here. Wait for pullback to 29500–29600 support before adding longs. Watch 4h candles for a reversal pattern. Better entry after NFP release if data surprises dovish (sub-200k jobs) or CPI cools. Short on retest of 30000 resistance if it fails to break decisively.

factors.name: Tech sector leadership
factors.summary: Nasdaq up 250 points (+0.97%) with semiconductor strength (SOXX +3%, NVDA +1.56%, AMD +3.45%, INTC +4.67%). Tech leads rally despite broader market hesitation in financials and consumer defensives.

factors.name: Broad index technicals
factors.summary: S&P 500 reclaimed 100-hour MA (7,443) and moved above 200-hour MA (7,463.23). NDX participation in upside suggests consolidation strength, not yet extended.

factors.name: AI capex narrative intact
factors.summary: OpenAI reports 50% inference cost reduction (bullish for AI adoption, neutral-to-bearish for memory chip demand per SOXX upside). AWS commits $1B to embedded AI engineers. Narrative supports hyperscaler reinvestment thesis.

factors.name: Fed policy uncertainty
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signals hawkish inflation-fighting stance at Sintra. Core inflation elevated; Fed's Hammack notes broad-based inflation concerns. Rate-hike bets linger despite no imminent action. Market pricing 26 bps by Feb 2027 (BoE commentary suggests low urgency).

factors.name: Real yields and duration sensitivity
factors.summary: No fresh 10Y or TIPS data in this window. Tech remains extremely duration-sensitive; any hawkish Fed surprise or inflation re-acceleration would compress NDX valuations. Current tone suggests modest upside risk to real rates.

factors.name: Consumer & labor market softness
factors.summary: US June consumer confidence came in at 91.2 vs 94.8 expected (expectations only +3.0). JOLTS job openings 7.594M vs 7.300M estimate (in line, no surprise). Labor market cooling narrative supports no-immediate-hike view but questions growth momentum.

factors.name: Retail investor sentiment on mega-caps
factors.summary: Citi notes retail activity in Mag 7 hit 4-year low in recent days (YOLO crowd moving away from tech giants). Structural pivot away from mega-cap tech could weigh on NDX composition.

factors.name: Geopolitical & macro cross-currents
factors.summary: US-Iran talks in Doha, Strait of Hormuz shipping gradual improvement, oil prices rolling over. Supreme Court rulings strengthen Trump's executive power but no direct tech impact. No immediate macro shock.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply chain
factors.summary: Keysight, WIN launching GaN MMIC design workflows; Vishay expanding protection portfolio. No supply-chain disruption signals. Baseline constructive for chip demand.

factors.name: Meta regulatory headwind
factors.summary: Meta loses bid to dismiss US states' claims on Facebook/Instagram child addiction, opening door to litigation costs. Antitrust / regulatory risk remains overhang on Mag 7 exposure.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is up 0.97% today with semiconductor strength (SOXX, NVDA, AMD, INTC outperformers) driving a broad tech rebound. The index reclaimed 100-hour and 200-hour moving averages, signaling short-term consolidation strength after recent chop. However, the move is still within Phase 2—not yet extended—and sentiment headwinds persist: retail hedge-fund-like positioning in Mag 7 has cooled to 4-year lows, consumer confidence disappointed, and Fed Chair Warsh is maintaining hawkish inflation rhetoric despite low labor-market urgency. Real-yield sensitivity remains a key risk if any inflation data re-accelerates or Fed forwards guidance reprices hawkish.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh remarks at Sintra policymaker forum (today/soon) — market watching for rate-hike confidence cues
FOMC meeting (late July 2026 assumed standard cycle) — inflation, rate path messaging critical for tech multiples
US CPI (early August expected) — core inflation print could re-trigger real-yield shock
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA likely late July / early Aug) — guidance on capex, cloud demand, AI monetization
US July jobs data (early August) — confirms or refutes labor-market cooldown narrative
China-US AI policy escalation risk — Trump admin AI crackdown on Anthropic could spread; geopolitical chip-export controls
screening.risk_warning: If Fed signals stronger hawkish tilt (via Warsh or any official) or inflation surprise re-accelerates, real yields could spike sharply, compressing Nasdaq valuations by 1-2% intraday. Retail sentiment pivot away from mega-caps could also trigger vacuum-collapse in low-volume sessions. Antitrust/regulatory overhang on META and other Mag 7 names could trigger single-name volatility that ripples to NDX. Any geopolitical escalation (US-China AI, Strait of Hormuz) could reverse safe-haven bid to equities.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway but not extended; wait for minor pullback to 26000-26050 support (100-hour MA zone) for lower-risk long entry. If resistance at 26200 breaks cleanly on volume, consider adds into 26250-26300. Avoid FOMO chase above 26300 given Phase 2 status and pending Warsh remarks—risk-reward unfavorable near session highs.

factors.name: Tech Mega-Cap AI Capex Announcements
factors.summary: Amazon announced $1B AI adoption push with direct engineer deployment (MarketWatch 17:41), AWS deploying FDEs to embed with customers. OpenAI cutting inference costs in half (The Information report 14:17), reducing capex pressure on hyperscalers.

factors.name: Semiconductor & AI Hardware Strength
factors.summary: AMD +3.45%, Intel +4.67%, SOXX semiconductor ETF +3% on positive chip demand signals. Sandisk upgraded by Bernstein (+5% intraday), signaling continued memory chip demand. NVDA position strong amid AI infrastructure buildout.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Bias (Hawkish Turn)
factors.summary: Cleveland Fed's Hammack stated 'AI could fuel inflation, rate hikes may be necessary' and 'inflation is still too high' (CNBC 15:31, 15:55). ECB's Rehn noted stagflationary effects from energy shock. Market pricing 26 bps BOE hikes by February.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Sensitivity
factors.summary: Higher nominal yields from hawkish Fed commentary compress long-duration tech multiples. 10Y Treasury benefiting from inflation concerns; real yields remain sticky despite Q2 rally. Mega-cap growth vulnerable if real rates sustain higher.

factors.name: Retail Tech Sentiment Deterioration
factors.summary: Citigroup reports retail investor activity in 'Magnificent Seven' hit 4-year low (14:49); YOLO crowd notably absent from mega-cap tech holdings after months of muted interest.

factors.name: Macro Resilience & Labor Market
factors.summary: Fed's Hammack notes jobs market 'right around full employment, growth looks good' (14:54). Canadian GDP beat forecasts at +0.5% April (15:19); US labor market stable despite inflation concerns.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness & Risk-Off Sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin below $60K, testing 2024 lows near $58,076 (16:49, 17:24). Strategy Inc. stock sliding as investors reverse confidence in Saylor's Bitcoin finance overhaul. Circle stock down 13% as stablecoin competition intensifies (14:32).

factors.name: Regulatory & Political Uncertainty
factors.summary: Meta loses bid to dismiss US states' addiction claims (17:13); Trump admin expands graduate loan caps; Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship. Mixed regulatory backdrop; no direct near-term NDX impact.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Carry Unwind
factors.summary: USD/JPY breaks 2024 highs above 162 (15:24), Brazilian Real at worst month as carry unwinds accelerate. Strong dollar headwind for US mega-cap international revenue (~40-50% for AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL).

factors.name: Recent Price Action: 4h +0.39%, 24h +0.40%
factors.summary: Modest intraday gains; S&P 500 reclaimed 200h MA at 7463 (15:24), Nasdaq +250 pts (+0.97%). Move is in progress but not extended; suggests Phase 2 positioning rather than Phase 3 exhaustion.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is grinding higher on AI capex bullishness (Amazon $1B, OpenAI cost cuts, semiconductor strength) but faces a hawkish Fed headwind—Cleveland Fed's Hammack explicitly warned rate hikes may be necessary if AI-driven inflation persists. Retail enthusiasm for mega-cap tech has collapsed to 4-year lows, and Bitcoin weakness ($58K) signals broader risk-off sentiment despite strong labor data. The move +0.4% in 24h is modest and still in Phase 2; upside capped by rising real yields and dollar strength eroding international earnings multiples. Sentiment dice favor a shallow consolidation into key support unless a major earnings beat (Nike after-bell, or upcoming AAPL/MSFT/NVDA) reignites animal spirits.
catalysts_ahead: Nike earnings after-bell today (16:00 ET) — key turnaround test
FOMC meeting minutes or Fed speakers (Warsh at Sintra, more hawkish commentary likely)
June NFP (early July release) — labor market validation or disappointment
CPI/PCE next week — inflation trajectory critical for rate-hike probability
AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA earnings (mid-July) — guidance on capex plans, cloud demand, AI revenue
screening.risk_warning: If Fed speakers (Warsh, others) shift dovish or inflation surprise lower, hawkish bias evaporates and NAS100 could spike 1–2%. Conversely, if CPI re-accelerates or Bitcoin continues free-fall below $55K, risk-off flows could trigger 2–3% pullback to 29400–29600. Nike miss would confirm retail/consumer malaise and trigger broad tech selloff. Monitor real yields (TIPS); if they break higher, duration compression accelerates.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway (+0.4% in 24h) in Phase 2; avoid chase above 30500 resistance. On dips toward 30000–30100 support, consider scaled BUY entries contingent on Nike earnings not triggering renewed risk-off; if Nike guidance is weak, wait for retest of 29600 before re-engaging bullish.

factors.name: Chip/AI Rally Continuation
factors.summary: Semiconductor stocks and AI names dominating YTD performance; TSMC/Samsung capex acceleration, chip tool stocks surging +9%, and hyperscaler infrastructure momentum remains intact per Bloomberg/MarketWatch reports.

factors.name: Q2 2026 Best Quarter in 6 Years
factors.summary: S&P 500 and Nasdaq set for best quarter since 2020 despite geopolitical risk; equities have added ~$8 trillion to S&P 500 value in 3 months per Bloomberg Markets.

factors.name: Labor Market & Economic Resilience
factors.summary: US jobs data showing strength, Canadian GDP beat (0.5% vs 0.4% expected in April), and consumer sentiment stable; supports earnings resilience for Nasdaq 100 components.

factors.name: Fed Rate-Hike Bets Emerging
factors.summary: Cleveland Fed's Beth Hammack stated inflation still too high and rate hikes may be necessary; markets pricing 26 bps in BoE hikes by Feb per FXStreet. This threatens long-duration growth multiples.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Real Yields
factors.summary: 10Y yields rebounded modestly in June rally; inflation expectations collapsed, but real yields remain structurally higher than early 2024. Tech multiple expansion may face headwinds if Fed inflation narrative hardens.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Yen Weakness
factors.summary: USD/JPY hit 40-year highs near 162.65 and broke above 2024 highs; broad USD strength despite month-end flows typically reduces valuations for mega-cap exporters (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL have significant international revenue).

factors.name: Crypto Volatility & BTC Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin fell below $60K to test Sept 2024 lows ($58K); BTC dropped 3%+ on MicroStrategy financing concerns. NQ-BTC correlation risk-off signal could trigger broader tech liquidation if sentiment sours.

factors.name: Microsoft Resistance & Chip Momentum Divergence
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports MSFT stuck below key resistance in bearish trend, suggesting largest NDX component facing distribution. Indicates caution despite chip rally; mega-cap concentration risk.

factors.name: Geopolitical & Energy Volatility
factors.summary: Oil fell for steepest quarterly drop in 6 years as Strait of Hormuz flows recover and Iran deal prospects improve; Middle East war risk fading but still presents tail-risk to sentiment.

factors.name: Antitrust & Regulatory Headwinds
factors.summary: Trump AI crackdown on Anthropic per US Top News; Circle stablecoin stock tanked on Open USD competition; regulatory uncertainty persists around mega-cap tech and AI governance.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 remains constructive on the back of a historic Q2 rally driven by chip/AI momentum, strong labor data, and economic resilience. However, the market is now caught between two conflicting narratives: (1) AI capex and semiconductor demand remain robust (TSMC, Samsung, chipmakers rallying), and (2) emerging Fed rate-hike chatter from Cleveland's Hammack and persistent inflation concerns threaten to compress long-duration growth multiples. The divergence between chip strength (up 570%+ in names like SanDisk YTD) and MSFT's struggle at resistance is a yellow flag. Dollar strength, BTC's drop to Sept 2024 lows, and the absence of mega-cap earnings catalysts in the immediate window further suggest a PHASE_2 consolidation rather than a breakout.
catalysts_ahead: Nike earnings after-hours today (6/30) — watch for China demand, tariff refunds, and guidance reset expectations
July 1 employment trends (month-end positioning may shift)
July FOMC meeting prep — market will front-run inflation narrative
Upcoming mega-cap earnings season (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN in late July/early Aug) — forward guidance on cloud capex, margins, and AI ROI critical
August PCE/CPI releases — will determine if Fed actually hikes or stays patient
China growth data and US-China trade/tech tensions (ongoing AI crackdown and chip export controls)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed speakers (Powell, other FOMC members) turn distinctly hawkish in the next 48-72h, or if PCE/CPI surprises hot, the bias flips to SELL and NAS100 faces 1-2% downside to 29700-30000. BTC breakbelow $58K would confirm risk-off and accelerate NDX pullback. Conversely, if AAPL/MSFT earnings beat with strong guidance and AWS capex commentary is bullish, the move could re-accelerate and break 31000.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 — upside momentum from Q2 rally is sustained but showing fatigue (MSFT resistance, BTC weakness, Fed chatter). Recommend waiting for a dip to 30100-30200 support before adding long positions; avoid chasing here. If breaks below 30000, reassess to SELL bias pending Fed/inflation commentary in next 48h.

factors.name: Q2 Earnings Momentum (Tech/Semis)
factors.summary: Nike crushed estimates; cybersecurity (Palo Alto, CrowdStrike) posting best quarters ever; chipmakers (Micron, Intel, AMD) added $2T combined value in Q2. Top 25 S&P 500 stocks dominated by semis/hardware (SanDisk +570% in 2025 on NAND/HBM/DRAM shortage colliding with AI demand).

factors.name: Q2 Index Performance Already Priced
factors.summary: Nasdaq already up 21.41% in Q2, Russell 2000 up 21.15%. Move is extended and quarter is ending; typical post-quarter profit-taking risk. NAS100 +0.07% in 4h and +0.48% in 24h signals very low momentum into close.

factors.name: AI Capex & Semiconductor Demand
factors.summary: AWS investing $1B into AI FDE unit; Anthropic launching drug discovery program; Google rolling out Gemini Spark on macOS. But export control headwinds on Anthropic models and Trump admin AI crackdown opening door for China.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Real Yields
factors.summary: Cleveland Fed President Hammack signaled AI could fuel inflation and rate hikes may be necessary. No major FOMC or inflation surprises in this window; market digesting cautious Fed commentary without fresh catalyst.

factors.name: Defense & Geopolitical Tailwinds
factors.summary: Lockheed Martin wins $3B radar contract; Northrop Grumman $68M defense contracts; AeroVironment skyrockets 19% on modernization/space spending. But crypto volatility (Trump disclosure of $1.2B crypto earnings, Binance lawsuit, Bitcoin dropped below $60K) and Iran nuclear tension creating cross-asset uncertainty.

factors.name: Valuation & Short Positioning
factors.summary: Michael Burry shorts Caterpillar for first time after 100% rally, also shorts NVDA and TSLA on valuation. Rising insider selling (La-Z-Boy VP, Ambarella director). Signals peak-cycle concern among sophisticated investors.

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Duration Sensitivity
factors.summary: 10-year and real yields not mentioned as moving sharply in this window. Gold down 11% for June despite brief move above $4K, indicating stable real-rate backdrop rather than yield shock.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & International Revenue
factors.summary: USD/CNH in tight range; DXY neutral near 101.20 with Fed rate hike bets alive. USD strength is modest headwind for Nasdaq names with heavy overseas revenue but not acute.

factors.name: Crypto Weakness & Risk-Off Signals
factors.summary: Bitcoin tested 2026 lows at $58,076 (within $41 of June 25 low), down from prior highs. Michael Saylor financing overhaul sell-off; Open USD stablecoin launch tanked Circle stock. NQ-BTC correlation suggests crypto selloff may drag NDX.

factors.name: Small-cap Outperformance & Rotation Signals
factors.summary: Russell 2000 +21.15% in Q2 (best start since 1991). Small-cap strength relative to mega-cap NDX is classic peak-cycle/growth-to-value rotation signal; suggests large-cap tech multiple compression risk.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq finished Q2 (+21.41%) with solid earnings from mega-cap tech, semis, and cybersecurity names, plus defense tailwinds from geopolitical spending. However, the move is already priced, insider selling is rising (Burry shorts NVDA/TSLA/CAT on valuation), and crypto weakness (Bitcoin test 2026 lows) is signaling risk-off undertones. Small-cap outperformance (Russell 2000 +21.15%) suggests rotation away from large-cap growth; the quarter-end momentum is fading with only +0.07% in the last 4 hours. AI capex remain strong but regulatory uncertainty (export controls on Anthropic, Trump admin AI crackdown) clouds forward guidance.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC minutes or next meeting guidance (typically July early in month)
July CPI/PCE data (late July — key driver of rate expectations)
Corporate guidance downgrades if guidance cut season begins
Anthropic / Open-source AI regulation updates (ongoing geopolitical/trade risk)
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META typically in July/early Aug)
US-Iran negotiations outcome (oil/risk premium impact)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) NVDA, MSFT, or GOOGL deliver surprise capex acceleration + robust hyperscaler order commentary; (2) Fed explicitly signals no rate hikes despite inflation talk (dovish repricing); (3) Real yields collapse (10Y TIPS yields <0.5%), turbocharging duration; (4) Bitcoin stabilizes and resumes uptrend (risk-on rewind); (5) Geopolitical de-escalation (Iran talks succeed, Trump restarts negotiations). Currently late in cycle with extended move; watch for mean reversion or confirmation of valuation reset.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended into Q2 close (PHASE_3). Do not chase higher. Wait for pullback to 30,050 support or reversal day close below 20-day MA before reconsidering long; short-term risk is profit-taking into month/quarter-end. Better entry on oversold bounce if 50-day MA tested (~29,800).

factors.name: Q2 2026 Performance & Chip Rally
factors.summary: Nasdaq posted +21.41% in Q2 2026, best quarter since 2020 (per MarketWatch). Record chip rally added $2T in combined value to Micron, Intel, AMD in H1 2026, suggesting sustained semiconductor/AI demand momentum beyond Nvidia.

factors.name: AI Capex & Hyperscaler Demand
factors.summary: AWS launching $1B AI unit to embed engineers with customers (per MarketWatch). Progress Software beat earnings, with CEO citing AI token-efficiency savings driving customer capex. No new pullback in cloud/AI spending signals continue.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Resilience
factors.summary: No supply chain disruptions or TSMC/ASML warnings in news. Export control relaxation (Fable/Mythos lift expected, per Politico) removes regulatory overhang; US-friendly AI regulation environment supports long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: ETF Inflows & Institutional Appetite
factors.summary: Investors poured record ETF inflows in H1 2026, with flows dominated by AI-linked stocks (per MarketWatch). QQQ and tech ETFs remain preferred vehicles; no sign of QQQ/XLK outflows or rotation to value.

factors.name: Real Yields & Long-Duration Sensitivity
factors.summary: No recent inflation surprises or Fed repricing mentioned in last 24h. Oil posted largest quarterly drop since pandemic (easing input/output price inflation per Investinglive), which supports compressed real yields. No 10Y Treasury yield data in window, but tone is deflationary.

factors.name: China & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: China factory PMI beat forecast at 50.3 (AI exports driving expansion), but domestic demand sputtering. US-Iran ceasefire talks progressing (VP Vance threatens force as backup option); oil volatility retreating, reducing tail risk on China supply chains.

factors.name: Upcoming Economic Data & FOMC Risk
factors.summary: China manufacturing PMI (Caixin/S&P Global) due tomorrow. No imminent US CPI, FOMC, or NFP in next 72h mentioned; this reduces near-term catalyst risk but also means no major tailwind. Consumer confidence and JOLTS data out of focus.

factors.name: Mega-cap Earnings Sentiment
factors.summary: Nike beat estimates, CrowdStrike & Palo Alto had best quarters ever on AI threat demand. No mega-cap earnings warnings or guidance cuts reported in this batch. Cybersecurity tailwind (CrowdStrike, Palo Alto) supports NDX tech/software mix.

factors.name: Crypto-Risk & Trump Positioning
factors.summary: Trump disclosed $1.2B+ in 2025 crypto income, $50M+ in BTC cold storage. While bullish for risk sentiment, no new regulatory tailwind for NDX tech stocks from this (crypto and NDX correlation is weak on fundamentals). Could signal risk-on flow spillover to equities.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Valuation Headwind
factors.summary: USD holding firm near 101.20 (DXY neutral, per FXStreet). While not spiking, strong Dollar pressures NDX international revenue (Apple, Microsoft, Google have ~40-50% non-US sales). No major USD breakdown to support multiple expansion.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq finished Q2 2026 with a stellar +21.41% gain, capping the best quarter since 2020 as chip stocks surged and AI capex momentum accelerated. Hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) continue heavy capex deployment; CrowdStrike, Palo Alto, and Progress Software all beat on AI-driven enterprise demand. Oil's 6-year quarterly decline has eased deflation fears, supporting long-duration growth multiples. However, the +0.48% 24h gain and modest +0.07% 4h move suggest momentum is consolidating—likely month-end positioning and technical pause before the next catalyst (China PMI tomorrow).
catalysts_ahead: China Caixin Manufacturing PMI (Wed 1 July, ~9:45 UTC) — signals domestic demand recovery vs. export reliance
US ISM Manufacturing PMI (Thu 2 July) — US capex/industrial strength data
ECB rate decision rumor (mentioned banking mini-tightening debate, dates unclear) — could influence USD/real yields
Mega-cap earnings: TSLA, AMZN, META, GOOGL if any pre-announce in next 7 days
Fed speakers or inflation-adjacent data (no CPI until early August expected)
screening.risk_warning: If China PMI disappoints tomorrow (sub-50), or if oil reprices sharply higher on Iran escalation (VP Vance's 'drop bombs' rhetoric could flip), real yields could spike and compress growth multiples. USD strength persists near 101.20—any break above 102 would pressure international-heavy NDX names (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL) disproportionately. Conversely, if no major data shock, NDX could consolidate sideways into month-end, reducing near-term upside conviction.
screening.entry_guidance: NAS100 is in early phase but not extended—suitable for measured long entries on pullbacks to 30000-30100 support or 4h retest of moving averages. Avoid chasing into current price; await China PMI reaction or a 30min pullback before adding longs. If moves above 30500, watch for extension into 31000, but reduce size given Q2 gains already priced in.
